domain: LLM mechanistic interpretability (induction head circuit) exported to neuroscience (Drosophila mushroom body)
specific_mechanism: the LLM induction-head two-head circuit predicts a specific functional motif in animal associative learning: sparse-coincidence-detection in step 1 then plasticity-modulated-readout in step 2, with the LLM's previous-token-attention-head ↔ KC sparse encoding and the LLM's induction-attention-head ↔ MBON dopamine-plasticity readout
llm_application: REVERSE DIRECTION — LLM circuit predicts a new neuroscience finding about which KC subpopulation participates in which MBON learning compartment; specifically predicts that connectivity-bias asymmetry (the 15-fold projection-neuron bias documented in bioRxiv 2025.10.29.684686) should correlate with induction-head-like position-tagging accuracy in the corresponding KC ensemble
novelty_claim: Using LLM-derived circuit motifs as predictive hypotheses for animal-neuroscience experiments is the reverse direction of the standard 'neuroscience inspires AI' framing. The specific bridge between induction-head structure and KC-MBON projection-bias is the testable hypothesis.
